channel.title: Oumar F. KEBE | Senior Data Product Manager | Expert IA & Gouvernance
channel.description: Recent content on Oumar F. KEBE | Senior Data Product Manager | Expert IA & Gouvernance

item.title: Mes Projets Data
item.description: Ajoutez ici vos projets GitHub, analyses Python ou tableaux de bord.

item.title: Mes Projets Data
item.description: Ajoutez ici vos projets GitHub, analyses Python ou tableaux de bord.

item.title: Introduction
item.description:
Hello World

A sample go program is show here.

package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
 message := greetMe("world")
 fmt.Println(message)
}

func greetMe(name string) string {
 return "Hello, " + name + "!"
}

Run the program as below:

$ go run hello.go
Variables

Normal Declaration:

var msg string
msg = "Hello"

Shortcut:

msg := "Hello"
Constants
const Phi = 1.618

item.title: Introduction
item.description:
Hello World

A sample go program is show here.

package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
 message := greetMe("world")
 fmt.Println(message)
}

func greetMe(name string) string {
 return "Hello, " + name + "!"
}

Run the program as below:

$ go run hello.go
Variables

Normal Declaration:

var msg string
msg = "Hello"

Shortcut:

msg := "Hello"
Constants
const Phi = 1.618

item.title: Basic Types
item.description:
Strings
str := "Hello"

Multiline string

str := `Multiline
string`
Numbers

Typical types

num := 3 // int
num := 3. // float64
num := 3 + 4i // complex128
num := byte('a') // byte (alias for uint8)

Other Types

var u uint = 7 // uint (unsigned)
var p float32 = 22.7 // 32-bit float
Arrays
// var numbers [5]int
numbers := [...]int{0, 0, 0, 0, 0}
Pointers
func main () {
 b := *getPointer()
 fmt.Println("Value is", b)

func getPointer () (myPointer *int) {
 a := 234
 return &a

a := new(int)
*a = 234

Pointers point to a memory location of a variable. Go is fully garbage-collected.

item.title: Basic Types
item.description:
Strings
str := "Hello"

Multiline string

str := `Multiline
string`
Numbers

Typical types

num := 3 // int
num := 3. // float64
num := 3 + 4i // complex128
num := byte('a') // byte (alias for uint8)

Other Types

var u uint = 7 // uint (unsigned)
var p float32 = 22.7 // 32-bit float
Arrays
// var numbers [5]int
numbers := [...]int{0, 0, 0, 0, 0}
Pointers
func main () {
 b := *getPointer()
 fmt.Println("Value is", b)

func getPointer () (myPointer *int) {
 a := 234
 return &a

a := new(int)
*a = 234

Pointers point to a memory location of a variable. Go is fully garbage-collected.

item.title: Flow Control
item.description:
Condition
if day == "sunday" || day == "saturday" {
 rest()
} else if day == "monday" && isTired() {
 groan()
} else {
 work()
}

if _, err := doThing(); err != nil {
 fmt.Println("Uh oh")
Switch
switch day {
 case "sunday":
 // cases don't "fall through" by default!
 fallthrough

 case "saturday":
 rest()

 default:
 work()
}
Loop
for count := 0; count <= 10; count++ {
 fmt.Println("My counter is at", count)
}

entry := []string{"Jack","John","Jones"}
for i, val := range entry {
 fmt.Printf("At position %d, the character %s is present\n", i, val)

n := 0
x := 42
for n != x {
 n := guess()
}

item.title: Flow Control
item.description:
Condition
if day == "sunday" || day == "saturday" {
 rest()
} else if day == "monday" && isTired() {
 groan()
} else {
 work()
}

if _, err := doThing(); err != nil {
 fmt.Println("Uh oh")
Switch
switch day {
 case "sunday":
 // cases don't "fall through" by default!
 fallthrough

 case "saturday":
 rest()

 default:
 work()
}
Loop
for count := 0; count <= 10; count++ {
 fmt.Println("My counter is at", count)
}

entry := []string{"Jack","John","Jones"}
for i, val := range entry {
 fmt.Printf("At position %d, the character %s is present\n", i, val)

n := 0
x := 42
for n != x {
 n := guess()
}

if day == "sunday" || day == "saturday" {
 rest()
} else if day == "monday" && isTired() {
 groan()
} else {
 work()
}

if _, err := doThing(); err != nil {
 fmt.Println("Uh oh")

 rest()
} else if day == "monday" && isTired() {
 groan()
} else {
 work()
}

if _, err := doThing(); err != nil {
 fmt.Println("Uh oh")

item.title: Bash Variables
item.description:
Variable
NAME="John"
echo $NAME
echo "$NAME"
echo "${NAME}
Condition
if [[ -z "$string" ]]; then
 echo "String is empty"
elif [[ -n "$string" ]]; then
 echo "String is not empty"
fi

item.title: Bash Variables
item.description:
Variable
NAME="John"
echo $NAME
echo "$NAME"
echo "${NAME}
Condition
if [[ -z "$string" ]]; then
 echo "String is empty"
elif [[ -n "$string" ]]; then
 echo "String is not empty"
fi

Je ne me contente pas de livrer des features Data : je transforme la complexité technique en levier de croissance business.

Senior Data Product Manager avec 6 ans d’expérience, j’opère à l’interface stratégique entre la DSI (Tech) et le Métier. Chez France Travail, je pilote l’industrialisation de produits Data critiques et la modernisation de la gouvernance (Doc-as-Code, Quality Gates).

Ma valeur ajoutée ? Une double compétence rare :

  • Vision Produit : Discovery, Roadmap RICE, et alignement des parties prenantes (C-Level).
  • Ancrage Technique : Capacité à auditer du code (Python, SQL), concevoir des architectures IA (RAG) et dialoguer d’égal à égal avec les développeurs.

Mon objectif : Dérisquer vos projets data/I.A. et maximiser leur adoption utilisateur.
